news.title: Johnson & Johnson’s Impella pumps miss the mark in 2 major coronary studies
news.snippet: Two separate randomized trials failed to illustrate Impella's benefits in specific cases of patients with blocked coronary arteries.

news.title: Astellas axes early Sjögren’s trial in latest setback for autoimmune disease community
news.snippet: Astellas Pharma has become the latest company to pull back from a potential treatment for Sjögren’s syndrome, an autoimmune disease that still lacks any dedicated medicines.

news.title: Blackstone closes record $6.3B life sciences fund
news.snippet: One of the world’s leading investment firms has broken its own record with the closing of its $6.3 billion Blackstone Life Sciences VI (BLXS VI) fund, which is 40% larger than its predecessor.

news.title: Merck dives into detailed phase 3 data showing PCSK9 pill easily outswims rivals
news.snippet: Merck & Co. has lifted the lid on the third successful phase 3 trial of its PCSK9 inhibitor, showing the edge the cardiovascular disease prospect has over other oral non-statin therapies.

news.title: Boston Scientific’s Watchman heart implant measures up to blood thinners in ACC trial
news.snippet: Boston Scientific’s Watchman implant cut bleeding risks almost in half compared to long-term anticoagulant regimens, while showing it was non-inferior in composite measures of stroke and death in peop

news.title: Kevin Tang uses newfound control of Aurinia to finally take over Kezar
news.snippet: Autoimmune specialist Aurinia, which named Kevin Tang as its new CEO last week, is acquiring Kezar after the latter company received promising feedback from the FDA. Tang previously made an unsuccessf

news.title: Fierce Biotech Layoff Tracker 2026: Takeda plots 634 US job cuts
news.snippet: As always, if you know of layoffs occurring at a biotech, please reach out to the Fierce Biotech editorial team.

news.title: Viridian’s other rival to Amgen’s Tepezza scores phase 3 win, but investors need convincing
news.snippet: Despite the primary endpoint hit, the candidate's inability to meet investors’ expectations cast a shadow over the results.

news.title: J&J secures $500M Royalty backing for autoimmune antibody program
news.snippet: Royalty Pharma has made another play in the autoimmune space, this time crowning Johnson & Johnson as the beneficiary. Royalty and J&J have agreed to co-fund the development of a chronic autoi

news.title: Connect’s Dupixent challenger hits the mark in phase 3 eczema trial
news.snippet: Connect Biopharma has reported 52-week improvements across multiple endpoints in a phase 3 trial of its eczema drug candidate, fueling the biotech’s belief that its molecule is better than Dupixent.

news.title: Predicting Clinical Seizure Liability Earlier in Drug Development
news.snippet: Central nervous system (CNS) toxicity accounts for 25% of safety failures across drug discovery and development, yet it is rarely detected during standard GLP toxicology studies1.

news.title: Scaling Radioligand Therapy: Expanding the Impact of Theranostics
news.snippet: Radioligand therapy has irrevocably changed oncology, proving we can map biology and strike cancer cells with unprecedented precision. Research suggests that up to 80% of cancers could potentially be

news.title: Kardigan hypertension drug tangles biomarker win with blood pressure miss in phase 2 trial
news.snippet: Kardigan’s tonlamarsen reduced a key biomarker for hypertension in a phase 2 trial, but missed the study’s other goal of significantly reducing blood pressure.

news.title: Biogen’s litifilimab sparkles in midphase lupus trial, raising hopes for pivotal readout
news.snippet: Biogen’s lupus drug candidate litifilimab has improved outcomes in another phase 2 trial, adding to the momentum behind a program barreling toward pivotal data in two forms of the autoimmune disease.

news.title: Kailera plots IPO to fund obesity pipeline after one of the biggest raises of 2025
news.snippet: Kailera Therapeutics—one of the biggest money-raisers of 2025—is now seeking to go public as it seeks further funding to push its obesity portfolio through the clinic.

news.title: BMS, Novartis, Gilead, Iovance dinged over biologics promos in rare spate of CBER untitled letters
news.snippet: Promotional materials for a handful of biologics have found their way into the FDA’s hot seat.

news.title: Lilly presses for UK deal that would see higher drug prices in exchange for resumed investments: FT
news.snippet: The company is looking to work out an arrangement with the U.K. government that would see higher drug prices in exchange for Lilly to continue its U.K. investments, according to a report in The Financ

news.title: United plots Tyvaso FDA filing after ph. 3 win elicits talk of 'new IPF standard' and blockbuster sales
news.snippet: With a second phase 3 win for Tyvaso in idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (IPF), United Therapeutics is padding the case for an expansion and putting more color on its filing plans with the FDA.

news.title: Regulatory tracker: GSK's long-acting Exdensur wins asthma nod in China
news.snippet: In this tracker, Fierce Pharma is recording the regulatory progress of in-market products, including expansions into key geographies and new indications. Some of these updates may not meet the bar for

news.title: Idorsia eyes pediatric insomnia use with midstage trial win for sleep med Quviviq
news.snippet: After notching a phase 2 trial win, Idorsia’s insomnia med Quviviq is one step closer to potentially becoming a first-in-class treatment for children.

news.title: Takeda begins US layoffs as part of massive $1.3B restructuring
news.snippet: Takeda has started a workforce reduction in the U.S., plotting to cut 634 jobs, as the company aims to save more than $1.26 billion in annual costs.

news.title: Samsung Biologics union gathers votes to strike as tension over wage, governance mounts
news.snippet: The labor union of Samsung Biologics has secured the votes needed to strike, signaling a potential escalation in an impasse over what the union has branded “[u]nresolved governance failures and rigid

news.title: Biogen looks to shake up SMA status quo with FDA nod for high-dose version of Spinraza
news.snippet: Biogen's Spinraza represented a major advance in treating SMA upon its FDA approval nearly a decade ago. It has since ceded some of its competitive positioning as new entrants made their way onto the

news.title: HCP engagement is stuck in the past: here’s how pre-consult precision unlocks what’s next
news.snippet: Forward-looking precision: How pharma can engage providers at the moments that shape care decisions

news.title: Beyond Pixel Lawsuits: How Pharma Marketers Can Turn Privacy Into a Performance Advantage
news.snippet: Why pharma marketers pulling back on tracking are seeing hidden costs, and what a more sustainable path forward looks like

news.title: #FierceMadness: Elite Eight emerge in 2026 Drug Name Tournament—VOTE NOW
news.snippet: It’s time once again for Fierce Pharma Marketing’s annual #FierceMadness tournament. This time around, we're watching 64 newly minted drug names go head to head.

news.title: ‘Health Doesn’t Need to Be Ludacris’: Bayer signs rapper-actor to multivitamin campaign
news.snippet: There’s no need to dive fast and furious into every single wellness trend, according to a Ludacris-fronted campaign from Bayer’s consumer health division.

news.title: Rocket plots measured trajectory for new gene therapy Kresladi after clearance to launch from FDA
news.snippet: Rocket has announced that the FDA signed off on an accelerated approval for its hematopoietic stem cell-based gene therapy, Kresladi, also known as marnetegragene autotemcel, to treat certain children

news.title: Pulse check on Lilly's GLP-1 fortunes
news.snippet: HSBC’s downgrade of Lilly is raising new questions about the GLP-1 market and the outlook for obesity treatments. The Fierce Pharma team breaks down what it could mean and what comes next.

news.title: Fierce Pharma Asia—Takeda’s $1.3B reorg; India’s GLP-1 floodgates; Gilead’s $2.2B buy of a China NewCo
news.snippet: Takeda is launching a restructuring, targeting nearly $1.3 billion in annual savings. Generic versions of Novo Nordisk's semaglutide reached India in chaotic fashion. Gilead Sciences is paying up to $

news.title: STAT+: Pharma giant Takeda to lay off nearly 250 workers in latest job cutting
news.snippet: Takeda Pharmaceuticals, the largest biopharma employer in Mass., will lay off 250 workers in Cambridge beginning in July.

news.title: STAT+: Doctors Without Borders calls Gilead ‘unconscionable’ for refusing to sell HIV prevention drug to organization
news.snippet: The move came after months of talks in which Doctors Without Borders asked Gilead Sciences for a “limited” supply of lenacapavir.

news.title: STAT+: Early signs of Alzheimer’s often go undetected. These researchers want to change that
news.snippet: Using AI to analyze changes in brain scans can predict Alzheimer’s with nearly 93 percent accuracy, scientists say.

news.title: STAT+: Biogen lupus drug improves skin clearance in mid-stage study
news.snippet: Insilico's large deal with Eli Lilly, Takeda's Phase 3 psoriasis data, and more biotech news from The Readout

news.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about Trump pushing drug pricing policy, Chinese biotech licensing, and more
news.snippet: The White House has drafted legislative text for its drug pricing policy and is sharing it with more than a dozen major pharmaceutical companies

news.title: STAT+: AI drug developer Insilico Medicine and Lilly ink commercialization deal worth up to $2.75 billion
news.snippet: Lilly has signed a deal with AI drug developer Insilico that’s worth $115 million up front and approximately $2.75 billion in biobucks

news.title: STAT+: Amid focus on food, FDA leader briefs lawmakers on priorities
news.snippet: Infant formula safety, updating labels, defining ultraprocessed foods, and expanding inspections of processing plants are among the priorities.

news.title: Ultrasound delays putting pregnant women and cancer patients at risk, sonographers say
news.snippet: Demand for ultrasound has increased but too few people are being trained for the job, sonographers warn.
